title: How Far Does Probe-Only Recalibration Transfer in Bayesian Quadrature?
description: This paper studies external recalibration of Bayesian quadrature intervals as a transfer problem rather than a new quadrature method. It uses a substantially larger pre-registered bank of analytic probes and pooled probe instances to compare simple variance-scaling and monotone roughness-aware inflation rules, selected entirely by probe-only cross-validation and evaluated under explicit probe-bank sensitivity tests.
motivation: Bayesian quadrature often delivers reasonable integral means while its reported posterior variances become overconfident under kernel misspecification, empirical-Bayes fitting, and local roughness. Existing literature mostly addresses this from inside the quadrature model, especially in Bayes-Sard and kernel-marginalization work, while earlier filtering papers already showed the importance of calibrating quadrature uncertainty; the gap is an honest stand-alone study of how far external recalibration can transfer without target labels and how sensitive that transfer is to the chosen probe bank.
proposed_approach: Run a shared GP-BQ backend on low-dimensional synthetic targets and on a fixed 72-probe analytic bank per measure. Pool calibration information across 48 calibration probes, 2 node budgets, and 3 design seeds to fit three pre-registered external recalibrators: global variance scaling, monotone isotonic inflation as a function of a scalar roughness diagnostic, and a small monotone stepwise rule. Choose among them using grouped leave-one-probe-out cross-validation, then evaluate on held-out probes, held-out targets, and first-class probe-bank sensitivity analyses including random sub-banks, leave-one-family-out transfer, and severity-range holdout.

related_work.title: Bayes-Hermite Quadrature
related_work.how_different: Foundational GP-based quadrature; the proposed paper does not alter the quadrature estimator and studies only post-hoc uncertainty correction.

related_work.title: Probabilistic Integration: A Role in Statistical Computation?
related_work.how_different: Frames probabilistic integration broadly but does not study probe-bank transfer for external recalibration of BQ intervals.

related_work.title: Convergence Analysis of Deterministic Kernel-Based Quadrature Rules in Misspecified Settings
related_work.how_different: Analyzes estimator robustness under misspecified smoothness, whereas this proposal targets finite-sample interval recalibration and transfer.

related_work.title: A Bayes-Sard Cubature Method
related_work.how_different: Improves robustness by changing the internal BQ prior and mean space; the proposed paper studies an external layer that keeps the BQ mean fixed.

related_work.title: Marginalising over Stationary Kernels with Bayesian Quadrature
related_work.how_different: Addresses kernel uncertainty inside BQ through model averaging; this proposal compares against it on a reduced subset but focuses on cheaper probe-only recalibration.

related_work.title: Bayesian Quadrature in Nonlinear Filtering
related_work.how_different: An early filtering-side precursor showing that accounting for quadrature uncertainty improves covariance credibility; the proposed work moves to stand-alone BQ and explicit transfer from probe banks.

related_work.title: Bayesian Quadrature Variance in Sigma-Point Filtering
related_work.how_different: Further studies variance estimation within sigma-point filtering, whereas the proposed work studies external recalibration without redesigning the internal quadrature rule.

related_work.title: Improved Calibration of Numerical Integration Error in Sigma-Point Filters
related_work.how_different: The closest conceptual precedent: it calibrates numerical integration error inside sigma-point filtering using Bayes-Sard structure, while this proposal is a stand-alone external recalibration transfer study.

related_work.title: Calibrating General Posterior Credible Regions
related_work.how_different: Provides a general posterior calibration perspective but not probe-only transfer for probabilistic integration.

related_work.title: A Bayesian Framework for Calibrating Gaussian Process Predictive Distributions
related_work.how_different: Studies post-hoc calibration for GP prediction rather than interval reliability for Bayesian quadrature integrals.

hypothesis: Pooled probe-bank recalibration can materially reduce Bayesian quadrature undercoverage under kernel misspecification, but simple global scaling is usually the most stable default and roughness-aware inflation only helps when the probe bank spans the target roughness regime; explicit probe-bank mismatch will reveal predictable degradation.
success_criteria: On the union of misspecified target families, at least one probe-only recalibration rule improves empirical 95% coverage over uncalibrated GP-BQ by at least 0.05 absolute.
The selected recalibration rule improves mean weighted interval score over uncalibrated GP-BQ while keeping median width inflation on matched smooth controls at or below 1.75x.
A roughness-aware rule beats global scaling on at least one misspecified family without substantial losses on the others.
Held-out probe results are directionally consistent with target-side gains in the matched-generator study.
Across 20 random half-bank recalibrations, the standard deviation of target 95% coverage remains below 0.04, indicating the result is not purely bank-fragile.
Leave-one-family-out and severity-range-holdout analyses show clear degradation under deliberate probe-target mismatch, making failure modes explicit rather than hidden.
The full experiment matrix fits within the 2-core CPU and 8-hour runtime budget.
